title: Five quick takeaways from the F1 Belgian GP

When it rains, it pours for George Russell
"Could be worse, could be much better," George Russell said about starting third in Spa, benefitting from Lando Norris' grid penalty but also being well behind team-mate and championship leader Kimi Antonelli, who snared another pole at the Ardennes loop.

summary:
George Russell suffered his latest gut punch in the 2026 Formula 1 season after contact with Lewis Hamilton ended his Belgian Grand Prix on lap 1. But the Briton was more "angry" due to the lack of battery charge that left him a sitting duck on the Kemmel straight than the contact with the Ferrari itself.

title: Carlos Sainz hit with grid penalty for F1 Belgian Grand Prix

Running in a podium position late in the race, Leclerc lost control into the barriers after three out of four brakes failed to work.
